When a user subscribes to your flows you have 24 hours to send as much free messages as you want. Soon as somebody subscribes or re-engages you have the 24 hour window opens and you can you can send as much messages back and forth as you want. It doesn't really matter what kind of messages they are the recipient probably does matter what kind of messages but just keep in mind that that is the point the 24 hour window actually opens. Now when a subscriber when the recipient actually interacts with your content in messenger within that 24 hour window 24 hour window the 24 hour will reset or it will move it will just keep moving forward. So example if I if you subscribe to my message flow messenger flow on Monday 12 p.m. and I send you a message six hours later and you reply to that message another six hours later that means now we're 12 hours in the 12 in the 24 hour window. Soon as you reply or engage with that message the 24 hour window will reset and restart at the 12th hour in the 24 hour rule. So from that 12th hour you have another 24 hours to send as many messages as you want so it keeps moving forward as long as people interact with your flows. If a subscriber doesn't interact with your messages the 24 hour window will close. If a subscriber does not interact with your messages 24 hours after your last message the 24 hour window is closed and you have only one option to re-engage that user.
